title: Students Take a 55-Mile Hike

source.title: New Jersey school requires freshmen to go on 55-mile hike

segments.title: Newark, New Jersey
segments.audioText: The New Jersey city of Newark is no national park.
segments.transcript: The New Jersey city of Newark is no national park.
segments.translationZh: 新澤西州嘅紐瓦克市並唔係咩國家公園。

points.label: The New Jersey city of Newark
points.explanationZh: 聽到 The...city of...，即係介紹城市位置；Newark 係城市名。

points.label: is no national park
points.explanationZh: is no... 係有少少強調嘅講法，意思係『絕對唔係』。

points.label: national park
points.explanationZh: national park 指國家公園；呢句用嚟對比城市同戶外環境。

segments.title: Outside Comfort Zones
segments.audioText: Yet here, of all places, is St. Benedict's Preparatory School, a private high school that requires students to step way outside their urban comfort zone.
segments.transcript: Yet here, of all places, is St. Benedict's Preparatory School, a private high school that requires students to step way outside their urban comfort zone.
segments.translationZh: 但偏偏就係呢度，有間 St. Benedict's Preparatory School，呢間私立高中要求學生大大走出佢哋嘅城市舒適圈。

points.label: Yet here, of all places
points.explanationZh: Yet 表示轉折；of all places 有『偏偏就喺呢個地方』嘅味道。

points.label: requires students to
points.explanationZh: require someone to do something 即要求某人做某事，新聞同學校規則常見。

points.label: step way outside
points.explanationZh: step outside 係走出；way outside 加強語氣，表示遠遠超出平時範圍。

points.label: urban comfort zone
points.explanationZh: urban 係城市嘅；comfort zone 係舒適圈，成組可以一齊記。

segments.title: A Freshman Tradition
segments.audioText: The program is a mandatory rite of passage for all freshmen.
segments.transcript: The program is a mandatory rite of passage for all freshmen.
segments.translationZh: 呢個計劃係所有中一／高中一年級新生都必須經歷嘅成長儀式。

points.label: mandatory
points.explanationZh: mandatory 即必須嘅、強制嘅；聽學校或公司規定時好常見。

points.label: rite of passage
points.explanationZh: rite of passage 指成長或人生階段嘅重要儀式／經歷。

points.label: for all freshmen
points.explanationZh: freshmen 係一年級新生；all 強調每一個都包括在內。

segments.title: After the Hike
segments.audioText: Some were bruised. All were weary.
segments.transcript: Some were bruised. All were weary.
segments.translationZh: 有啲人瘀傷咗。所有人都好攰。

points.label: Some were...
points.explanationZh: Some were... 講『有啲人係...』；新聞結尾常用短句製造畫面。

points.label: bruised
points.explanationZh: bruised 指有瘀傷；可以形容身體受咗碰撞。

points.label: All were weary
points.explanationZh: weary 比 tired 更書面少少，意思係疲累、筋疲力盡。

sentences: The New Jersey city of Newark is no national park.
Yet here, of all places, is St. Benedict's Preparatory School, a private high school that requires students to step way outside their urban comfort zone.
The program is a mandatory rite of passage for all freshmen.
Some were bruised. All were weary.

vocabulary.example: The city is not a national park.
vocabulary.translationZh: 呢個城市唔係國家公園。

vocabulary.example: It is a private high school in Newark.
vocabulary.translationZh: 呢間係紐瓦克一間私立高中。

vocabulary.example: The school requires students to go hiking.
The students step outside their urban comfort zone.
vocabulary.translationZh: 學生走出佢哋嘅城市舒適圈。

vocabulary.example: The hike is mandatory for freshmen.
vocabulary.translationZh: 呢次遠足係一年級新生必須參加嘅。
所有一年級新生都參加呢個計劃。

vocabulary.example: After the hike, all the students were weary.
vocabulary.translationZh: 遠足之後，所有學生都好攰。

phrases.meaningZh: 偏偏喺呢個地方；竟然係呢度
phrases.example: Yet here, of all places, is the school.
The hike helps students step outside their comfort zone.

phrases.meaningZh: 成長儀式；人生階段嘅重要經歷
phrases.example: The program is a rite of passage for freshmen.
Some were bruised. All were weary.

speakingPractice.instructionZh: 由 00:04 跟讀第一句，留意 Newark is no national park 嘅重音落喺 no national park。
重播 00:18，先聽 Yet here, of all places，再補 requires students to step way outside。
用 step outside your comfort zone 練替換：This project helped me step outside my comfort zone.
用兩句英文複述：The school sends freshmen on a long hike. The program helps them work together.

notes: 今日重點係聽 Steve Hartman 式嘅故事型新聞，句子有畫面感，但可以逐段拆開聽。
先用時間碼聽 00:04、00:18、01:12、02:38；第二次先睇 transcript，特別留意 Yet here, of all places。
mandatory、rite of passage、comfort zone 都係新聞同日常都會遇到嘅實用字組。
dailyLifePractice 會配合戶外活動同約時間，練確認安排、問路同解釋小延誤。

dailyLifePractice.title: Confirming a Meet-Up
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你同朋友約咗去行山或戶外活動，要自然確認集合安排。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Are we still meeting at eight? I can meet you by the trail entrance. Text me when you park.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Are we still meeting at eight?

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I can meet you by the trail entrance.
lines.zh: 我可以喺行山徑入口附近等你。

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.en: Sounds good. I'll text you when I park.
lines.zh: 好呀。我泊好車就短訊你。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: Are we still meeting...? 係美國日常好自然嘅確認句。by the trail entrance 即入口附近，唔使講到非常精確。

drills.label: Substitution
drills.prompt: 將 eight 換成 nine-thirty、after lunch 或 Saturday morning。
drills.example: Are we still meeting after lunch?

drills.label: Quick repeat
drills.prompt: 跟讀 I can meet you by the trail entrance 三次。
drills.example: I can meet you by the trail entrance.

dailyLifePractice.title: Asking About the Route
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你第一次去某條路線，想問清楚會唔會難行。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Is this trail beginner-friendly? I brought water, but I don't have hiking boots. Should I be worried?

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.en: Mostly, but there are a few steep parts.
lines.zh: 大致啱，不過有幾段比較斜。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I brought water, but I don't have hiking boots.
lines.zh: 我帶咗水，不過冇行山鞋。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Good to know. I just wanted to check.
lines.zh: 知道就好。我只係想確認吓。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: beginner-friendly 好口語又實用，意思係適合新手。I just wanted to check 用嚟柔和語氣，表示你唔係質疑，只係想問清楚。

drills.label: Say-it-your-way
drills.prompt: 用 Is this... beginner-friendly? 問另一件事。

drills.label: Short response
drills.prompt: 用 Good to know 回應對方提供嘅資料。
drills.example: Good to know. I just wanted to check.

dailyLifePractice.title: Explaining a Small Delay
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你去集合地點途中遇到交通慢，要簡短通知朋友。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: I'm running a little behind. Traffic is moving slowly, but I'm close. Start without me if you need to.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Traffic is moving slowly, but I'm close.
lines.zh: 交通行得慢，不過我近喇。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Start without me if you need to.
lines.zh: 如果需要，你哋可以唔等我先開始。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: I'm running a little behind 比 I'm late 柔和，適合預早通知。Start without me if you need to 聽落體貼，唔會令對方有壓力。

drills.label: Polite rephrase
drills.prompt: 用 I'm running a little behind 開頭，換一個原因。
drills.example: I'm running a little behind. The bus is taking longer than expected.

drills.label: Role-play
drills.prompt: 朋友問 Are you close? 你用一句簡短英文回答。
drills.example: Yes, I'm about five minutes away.
